title: Client Engineer - Star Trek Fleet Command
description: Scopely is looking for a Client Engineer to join our Star Trek Fleet Command team within our Live Games Portfolio division in Dublin. At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day \- whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily. Star Trek Fleet Command is a standout success in the gaming industry. Launched in 2018, our in\-house developed 4X Strategy MMO has continually evolved, earning its place as one of the top\-grossing games on the market. ### **What You Will Do** As a Client Engineer focused on Core Tech and Platform Engineering, you’ll contribute to the ongoing evolution and stability of our Unity\-based client. This role focuses on the systems, tooling, and infrastructure that support the game experience across devices and platforms. You will work closely with senior engineers and cross\-functional teams to improve performance, maintainability, and scalability across the client technology stack. * Contribute to the development and maintenance of shared client systems such as asset loading, UI systems, tooling, memory tracking, patching, and performance monitoring. * Help identify and resolve performance, stability, and scalability issues across the client. * Collaborate with Backend, DevOps, QA, and gameplay teams to improve workflows, integrations, and development processes. * Follow and contribute to coding standards, architectural patterns, and engineering best practices. * Support Unity upgrades and platform integrations across Android, iOS, and PC. * Assist in improving the modularity, reliability, and maintainability of the codebase. * Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and collaborative problem\-solving. ### **What We're Looking For** * 3\+ years of professional experience with Unity and C\# development. * Experience working on game systems, shared frameworks, tools, or platform\-related features. * Solid debugging and problem\-solving skills, including investigating performance and memory\-related issues. * Understanding of Unity systems such as AssetBundles, scene management, rendering, or lifecycle management. * Experience developing for mobile platforms (iOS/Android) and familiarity with profiling/debugging tools such as Unity Profiler, Android Profiler, or Xcode Instruments. * Familiarity with version control systems and CI/CD workflows such as Git, Jenkins, or GitHub Actions. * Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across disciplines. * BSc degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience. ### **Bonus Points** * Experience supporting Unity version upgrades or large\-scale client migrations. * Exposure to performance optimisation and memory management techniques. * Familiarity with networking technologies such as WebSockets, Protobuf, or gRPC. * Experience with native plugins or platform\-specific integrations. * Knowledge of LiveOps systems, telemetry, or experimentation frameworks. * Understanding of backend\-client coordination and data synchronisation. * Exposure to mobile development challenges such as device fragmentation, app store requirements, or patching systems. At Scopely, we create games for everyone and we want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us \& embrace the adventure! ### **About Scopely** Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all\-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live\-operates one of the most diversified and award\-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play. Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world\-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet\-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the \#1 mobile games company in the U.S. and \#2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come. Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play. Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/. Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com. Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice. Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

title: Cloud Security Analyst
description: **Job Title: Cloud Security ANalyst** **Job Description** 🧑‍💼 Reporting to: Senior IT Security Manager **🚀 About The Role** We’re looking for a hands\-on **Cloud Security Analyst** with deep expertise in cloud\-native and container security. This role sits at the core of securing large\-scale, multi\-cloud and Kubernetes\-driven environments. You will play a critical role in defining security standards, implementing hardened baselines, and enhancing detection and response capabilities—working closely with DevOps, Networking, and SOC teams to embed security into every layer of design and automation. **🧩 Key Responsibilities** * Strengthen and optimize cloud\-native security controls across IAM, network security (WAF, VPC), and data protection (KMS, Vault) * Deploy and manage CSPM, CNAPP, and CWPP solutions to maintain continuous security posture across multi\-cloud and Kubernetes environments * Integrate cloud telemetry (GCP Audit Logs, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Kubernetes audit logs) into SIEM/SOAR platforms for centralized visibility * Define and enforce cloud security architectures and guardrails aligned with Zero Trustand Least Privilegeprinciples * Develop and maintain Security\-as\-Codepractices using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Bicep, including policy\-as\-code frameworks * Own and enhance the security posture of containerized environments (GKE, AKS, EKS), including image, registry, and runtime security * Harden Kubernetes clusters through RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and secure configurations * Lead cloud security incident response activities including triage, containment, and forensic investigations * Continuously monitor emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack vectors within cloud\-native ecosystems **Experience** **🎯 Required Qualifications** * 5\+ years in Information Security * Minimum 3\+ years in Cloud Security * At least 2\+ years working with Kubernetes / container security **Cloud Expertise** * Hands\-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (GCP and Azure preferred; AWS/OCI a plus) **Container Kubernetes Security** * Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) * Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration **Technical Skills** * Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) * Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation **Security Frameworks Compliance** * Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks **Certifications (Preferred)** * CISSP * CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) * GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer * Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect * AWS Certified Security – Specialty **Soft Skills** * Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams * Experience working in global or multi\-national environments **⭐ Nice to Have** * Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures * Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) * Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations * Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI * Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused crede

title: Commercial Account Executive (DACH)
description: **About Datadog:** Datadog is a best\-in\-class SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth, profitability, and stock market appreciation. Built by engineers, for engineers, our monitoring and security platform is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries to enable digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stack. We’re dedicated to creating, developing, and supporting our product and customers, allowing for seamless collaboration and problem\-solving among Dev, Ops, and Security teams globally. Come join the team for an exciting opportunity to learn from top\-level leaders and colleagues and grow alongside the company as we rapidly expand. **The Team:** Our sales team works with a best\-of\-breed product that solves real problems for our customers. Sellers follow a well\-defined methodology that helps them identify the customer's unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. Whether you're looking to learn from the best or be the best, the Datadog sales team is dedicated to furthering personal development and team success. **The Opportunity:** Datadog is the monitoring and analytics platform for developers, IT teams and business users in the cloud age. We're scaling our Inside Sales team to help IT and Technology leaders recognize Datadog’s impact in their digital transformation and migration to the cloud. This is an opportunity to bring a proven product, loved by thousands of customers, to a multi\-billion dollar market. **You Will:** * Focus 100% on net new logo acquisition * Manage the full sales cycle * Run and partner with Sales Engineers on demos * Use tools such as Sales Navigator and ZoomInfo * Strategically prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT Leaders, \& technical end users **You Are:** * Focused on hunting and closing net new logos * A top performer with a history of success * Bold on the phones, and creative in your emails * Able to strategically map out and break into accounts * Motivated, curious, hungry, and driven as a sales person * Fluent in German **Bonus Points:** * Passionate about tech and the cloud space * Experience with SaaS/Cloud and Salesforce **Why You Should Apply:** * Continuous career development and pathing opportunities * Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message * Product training to develop an in\-depth understanding of our product and space * Best in breed onboarding * Internal mentor and buddy program cross\-departmentally * New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan * President’s Club for top performers * Generous and competitive benefits * Friendly and inclusive workplace culture * Healthy work/life balance with discretionary unlimited paid time off **About Datadog:** Datadog is the leading observability and security platform for the AI era, providing businesses with unified visibility across the technology stack to manage complexity at scale. It brings applications, infrastructure, data, models, and security into one place, using AI to detect and resolve issues before they impact customers. Trusted globally by Fortune 500 companies and high\-growth AI leaders, Datadog enables businesses to move faster with clarity and confidence. Learn more about \#DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center. **Equal Opportunity at Datadog:** Datadog is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference. Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete this form. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications. **Privacy and AI Guidelines:** Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice. For information on our AI policy, please visit Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines.

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Polish Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Solution Engineering Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both solution engineering and sales. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Solution Engineering** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned an experienced **Solution Engineering Buddy** who will provide hands\-on coaching and involve you in some of their live deals. You will also be paired with experienced **Sales Development Buddies** during the Sales Rotation and you also get to call your first salesforce leads and find your first deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior solution engineering and sales professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) * For the Solution Engineering Path, we consider applicants from the following academic backgrounds: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Software Engineering, Data Science/Data Engineering, Information Systems, or other adjacent disciplines * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English and Polish What does our new EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, you will experience both the **Solution Engineering Path** and the **Sales Path** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Solution Engineering** , you will then join the **Sales Graduate Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will gain exposure to both roles. The time spent mastering the fundamentals of these complementary disciplines will enable you to be more productive and impactful upon promotion. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organization, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view Sales Graduate Rotation * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organization What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Solution Engineering organization * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Solution Engineering Path** , you can continue to progress: * After completing the Graduate Program rotations, you may progress to Associate Solution Engineer, with opportunities to work across ESB, SMB, and GRB customer segments, or join our Specialist SE team. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Company Secretary
description: **The Role** An excellent law firm in Dublin is looking for a dynamic and skilled **Corporate Company Secretary** to join their growing team. Unlike traditional transactional roles, this position is **distinctly advisory\-led and client\-facing** . You won't just be processing documents; you will be acting as a trusted consultant to a diverse portfolio of clients, guiding them through complex governance landscapes. This is a collaborative environment where technical grounding meets high\-level client engagement, offering significant opportunities for career development and exposure to top\-tier professional services. **What You Will Be Doing** * **Client Advisory:** Acting as the primary point of contact for clients, providing expert advice on Company Secretarial matters and corporate governance best practices * **Board Support:** Managing the full cycle of board support, including attending meetings, taking high\-quality minutes and drafting complex resolutions * **Statutory Compliance:** Ensuring all clients meet their statutory and regulatory requirements under Irish and UK law * **Governance Leadership:** Keeping abreast of legal and regulatory changes and proactively advising clients on the implications for their business * **Portfolio Management:** Overseeing the maintenance of company registers and records with meticulous attention to detail * **Mentorship:** Supporting and helping develop more junior members of the team **About You** * Roughly **3–5 years** of experience as a Company Secretary in professional services in Ireland * You must have a proven track record of advising clients directly, rather than just performing administrative or transactional tasks * A strong technical grounding in Irish/UK company law to ensure you can discuss complex work credibly with stakeholders * Exceptional interpersonal skills with the "client\-ready" presence required to represent the firm at a high level * Ideally, CGI qualified or currently pursuing professional memberships **Benefits \& Perks** * **Competitive Salary:** €50k– €65k depending on experience * **Hybrid Working:** A stable 2/3 split (2 days in\-office), providing flexibility from day one * **Professional Growth:** Support for CGI/professional qualifications and memberships * **Environment:** A supportive, collaborative culture within a modern Dublin office **This role is ideal for a CoSec professional looking to move away from the "back office" and into a front\-facing, advisory capacity. If you are ready for a new challenge in a premier legal environment, please get in touch!**

title: Compliance Administrator
description: **Akeso Healthsearch (formerly AGC Healthcare) is seeking to recruit a Compliance Administrator.** Akeso Healthsearch is a leading provider of healthcare recruitment services in Ireland connecting healthcare professionals with public and private clients for permanent placements, contracts and locum. We focus on helping healthcare professionals and providers thrive, as people are our purpose. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast\-growing team with excellent career progression and development prospects. Our company has adapted to the hybrid working model, which gives our employees the combination of onsite and offsite work. The Compliance Administrator has a key role in ensuring that the compliance of our healthcare professionals are completed in line with the legislation and HIQA standards. **Requirements:** * Ideally have experience in a similar role. * In\-depth knowledge of employment legislations, immigration law and relevant legislation in the areas outlined below. * Attention to detail is a pre\-requisite * Ability to work on your own initiative * Strong administration skills * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * Strong interpersonal skill * Ability to prioritize your workload * Sense of urgency and ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment * Self\-motivated and driven **Responsibilities:** * Maintaining appropriate compliance manuals, policies and procedures that meet regulatory requirements. * Manage garda vetting and compliance of Healthcare Professionals. * Maintain accurate records in line with legislation and HIQA standards. * Assisting Healthcare Professionals registering with the required regulatory bodies. * Assisting Healthcare Professionals with their application for validation and recognition of non\-Irish qualification. * Processing of employment visas and work permits. * Reference checks Akeso Healthsearch is an equal opportunities employer and offers an excellent remuneration package for the successful candidate.

title: Construction Site Engineer
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland's leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. ****Site Engineer (Mechanical)**** ****Location:**** Mobile (Cavanhill and Greenmount, Co. Louth will be initial projects) Duration: Permanent ****Work Hours/Schedule:**** Monday to Friday, 7\.30am to 4\.30pm ****Overview of the Role:**** Due to continued success and growth across the Water division, we are looking for an enthusiastic and hardworking individual to join the ECI (Early Contractor Involvement) Team as a Site Engineer. The successful candidate will work on a wide scope of Water and Wastewater Treatment projects across the Island of Ireland. The successful candidate will work alongside the Project Manager in coordinating a multi disciplined team to include process/mechanical/civil/structural/electrical work associated with various Water and Wastewater Treatment projects across the Island of Ireland. The Site Engineer will have responsibility for assisting in the on\-site delivery and construction of water and wastewater treatment contracts from detailed design stage through to installation, testing and commissioning. Collaborating with the Project Manager in the implementation of delivery documentation including technical submissions, managing sub\-contractor activities on site, ensuring regulatory Health \& Safety compliance, overseeing installation works, and coordinating construction activities. During the delivery stage the Site Engineer will liaise with design teams and suppliers, manage subcontractors, installation personnel and commissioning teams on site to ensure that projects are delivered on schedule and within budget. Ensure all equipment is delivered, installed, tested and ultimately commissioned in accordance with contractual requirements. ****Day to Day of the Role:**** * Take ownership of delegated site activities and project issues * Ensure thorough knowledge and understanding of the contract for all assigned projects * Ensure adherence to Veolia's QHSE standards on site * Liaison with Client and Client Representatives on site matters * Communicate with the Project Manager, Design Leads and Sub\-Consultants during the execution of the project * Interact with our multi\-disciplined engineering team, overseeing equipment installation and commissioning activities on site * Organising, co\-coordinating and managing site activities while maintaining H\&S in line with Veolia standards * Managing aspects of the project within defined budgets and agreed timelines through monitoring and tracking for adherence with the project brief and programme * Apply procedures in place related to contract execution and site operations * Provide site supervision cover for the construction site manager in the event of their absence * Manage all site\-based operatives, overseeing all aspects of their work including carrying out safety inductions, tool box talks, controlling the quality of work, ensuring method statement and order fulfilment, cleanliness and tidiness, the use of plant and tools, the reduction of waste and use of PPE ****Required Skills for the Role:**** * Minimum NFQ Level 7 degree in an Engineering (Mechanical Engineering Degree is preferable) and/or Science Discipline * Practical site experience (Essential) * Practical experience in being part of a project team from design stages to delivery stage * Managing site activities including team coordination * Ability to work on own initiative and effectively manage \& coordinate site activities * Experience in the execution of water/wastewater engineering projects is desirable * IT skills such as Microsoft Word, Excel, Project, Power Point * Good organisational and time management skills * Self\-motivated * Strong communication and writing skills **Please note:** ​If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website. Here at Veolia, we're proud to be known as an equal opportunities employer. We know how important it is to be fully committed to building and maintaining a diverse and inclusive place to work for every one of our colleagues. If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining work permits, is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Corporate Action Analyst French Language
description: **Role Title: Corporate Action Analyst French Language** **Role Designation:** Process Executive **Reporting Manager:** Team Lead **Job Level:** 2B **Location:** Dublin **About Infosys BPM Ltd** Infosys BPM, the business process management subsidiary of Infosys, provides end\-to\-end transformative services for clients across the globe. Our integrated IT and BPM solutions approach enables us to unlock business value across industries and service lines, addressing business challenges for clients. We leverage innovative business excellence frameworks, productivity improvements, process reengineering, automation, and cutting\-edge technology platforms to help clients achieve cost reduction objectives, improve process efficiencies, enhance effectiveness, and deliver superior customer experience. title: Cost Accountant
description: **Company Overview:** The O’Reilly Group comprises three companies: O’Reilly Precast, O’Reilly Barleystone, and O’Reilly Oakstown. O'Reilly Precast is a leading manufacturer of precast concrete products in the UK and Ireland. With a focus on innovative and efficient modular building solutions, we have established ourselves as market leaders in the construction industry. Our commitment to quality, reliability, and customer satisfaction sets us apart in the industry. **Job Description:** We are seeking a detail\-oriented and analytical Cost Accountant to join our team in the O’Reilly Group. This role involves supporting financial visibility by reviewing project costs, recording stock, assessing the cost impact of remakes and enhancing cost accounting processes. The successful candidate will occasionally visit manufacturing sites during stock takes and operational reviews, working closely with production teams and site staff to gain a strong understanding of processes. **Responsibilities:** * **Group Product Costing:** Maintain costs for group products manufactured, and analyse changes in product design, raw materials, labour, and manufacturing methods on overall product cost. * **Precast Project Review Analysis:** Maintain and review project revenue and costs versus budget, identifying variances and supporting corrective actions where required. * **Inventory Management:** Review stock takes, monitor stock count procedures, reconcile inventory variances, and assist with inventory valuation processes. * **Support Functions:** Provide support for production system (MPM) and project management system (PMP). * **Data Analysis \& Reporting**: Analyse large volumes of production and financial data using Excel, including the use of formulas, pivot tables, lookups, and reporting tools to identify trends, variances, and cost\-saving opportunities. * **Cost accounting systems**: Drive the redesign and automation of cost accounting processes to improve efficiency. * **Variance Analysis:** Prepare and review material usage, labour, overhead, and production variance reports, highlighting key issues impacting profitability and operational efficiency. * **Remake review\-** Work with the Quality department to maintain list of re\-makes and quantify costs. * **Cross\-Functional Collaboration:** Liaise with Production, Engineering, Quality, and Finance teams to support continuous improvement initiatives and cost control measures. **Requirements:** * Qualification in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related discipline, or currently working towards a professional accounting qualification (ACCA, CIMA, ACA, CPA or equivalent). * Advanced Microsoft Excel skills essential. * Experience in cost accounting within a manufacturing or construction environment preferred. * Ability to interpret cost data and provide actionable insights. * Strong organisational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. **How to Apply:** Please send your CV and cover letter to hr@oreilly.group. *O'Reilly Group is an equal opportunities employer. All qualified applicants are encouraged to apply.* Job Type: Full\-time Work Location: In person Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Gym membership * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience working with inventory, stock control, or manufacturing costing? * Do you hold a degree or qualification in Accounting, Finance, or a related field? * Are you highly experienced in using Microsoft Excel? Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Data Architect
description: **Deloitte is the biggest professional services Firm in the world and making an impact is more than just what we do: it’s why we’re here. We’re driven to create positive progress for our clients, community, people, and the planet. This sense of purpose inspires us to work to the highest standards, to tackle the challenges that matter.** *Joining us means becoming part of a Consulting team that’s at the top of its game and growing. Working with the best people and technology in the market, you’ll tackle meaningful projects that transform the future for high\-profile local and international clients. And you’ll do it as part of a people\-first culture that’s supportive, collaborative and, above all, welcoming.* At Deloitte, our Artificial Intelligence \& Data Team drives innovation by transforming data into actionable insights that empower our clients. As a Data Architect, you will play a pivotal role in designing and delivering cutting\-edge data solutions across diverse industries, leveraging the latest technologies and our global network. This role offers a unique opportunity to lead impactful projects, collaborate cross\-functionally, and develop your leadership skills in a dynamic, supportive environment focused on continuous learning and personal growth. **Key Skills And Prior Experience** * A minimum of a third\-level degree in Maths, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline * Proven experience leading and delivering complex data\-related projects, with the ability to motivate and guide teams to achieve outstanding results * Strong stakeholder management skills, including influencing senior leaders and providing expert advice based on industry best practices * Commercial experience in information and data management, with familiarity in enterprise\-grade data architecture technologies such as Cloudera CDP, Azure Databricks, and cloud\-native environments **What type of work will I be doing?** * Scoping, designing, and leading the implementation of scalable data solutions and models using open source and proprietary technologies * Developing technical specifications and architecture designs for data storage and management aligned with enterprise standards * Ensuring data security and integrity are embedded within all architectural designs * Leading and mentoring data analysts and engineers to deliver solutions aligned with target architectures * Collaborating across Deloitte’s Technology and Transformation business areas to integrate data capabilities with broader organisational goals * Managing client engagements across various industries, building trusted relationships and identifying growth opportunities * Coaching junior team members, aligning their responsibilities with their professional development objectives **Who is the Hiring Manager?** The hiring manager is an experienced leader within Deloitte’s Artificial Intelligence \& Data Team, known for a collaborative and inclusive leadership style. They value open communication, professional development, and fostering a positive team culture. title: Data Centre Shift Operations - Electrical AP
description: **Join us, be part of more.** We’re so much more than an energy company. We’re a family of brands revolutionising how we power the planet. We're energisers. One team of 21,000 colleagues that's energising a greener, fairer future by creating an energy system that doesn’t rely on fossil fuels, whilst living our powerful commitment to igniting positive change in our communities. Here, you can find more purpose, more passion, and more potential. That’s why working here is \#MoreThanACareer. We do energy differently \- we do it all. We make it, store it, move it, sell it, and mend it. **About your team:** At Centrica Business Solutions, we’re proud to partner with over 7,000 organisations globally – helping them to balance planet, with profit. We build, operate and maintain onsite, large\-scale energy assets like Solar PV and Combined Heat and Power – to help businesses to save the planet and save money. We’re also transforming the UK’s energy landscape by partnering with landowners and developers to build a 900MW portfolio of new grid\-scale solar farms and battery storage assets. We have a great opportunity for a **Shift Operations Electrical AP** for a key role in our Data Centre Operations, which provides best in class operation and maintenance for our customers critical power generation sites. The role focuses on system monitoring, implementation of procedures and SSOW, equipment checks and system maintenance and repairs for the client equipment. The role would suit a candidate who possesses the skills necessary to effectively interpret data from multiple sources, react promptly to event notifications and follow established procedures to rectify and escalate issues, in line with contractual obligations. **About the role:** * Monitoring customer assets to maximise efficiency in line with contractual terms * Managing and prioritising incoming event notifications to comply with relevant contractual obligations * Providing professional, concise communications to a variety of internal and external customers and stakeholders * Maintain accurate records of all tasks undertaken on the monitoring tool and asset management system. * Ensuring a comprehensive and thorough handover takes place between shift teams to maintain continuity of safe operations and ensure ongoing knowledge of work activities. * Working efficiently in an SLA (Service Level Agreement) driven team environment * Daily site inspections of mechanical \& electrical engineering systems and technical equipment. * Ensure the site's environmental conditions provide a safe work environment. * React to Building Management System alarms, and complete emergency actions as set out in the agreed procedures. * Issue permits and oversight of third\-party vendors, ensuring compliance with company\-developed processes, procedures, and all applicable laws and regulations. * Producing reports detailing asset availability and performance patterns * Adhering to all applicable company policies, procedures and business ethics codes * Provide support to the maintenance team during maintenance activities. **Here's what we're looking for:** * Previous experience working in a mission critical and/or power generation environment. * Good IT literacy, ability to write and develop documents, SOPs/EOPs. * A good understanding of CMMS (Maintenance Management Systems) is desirable but not essential. * Familiar with UPS \& battery systems. * Experience in carrying out electrical switching activities \& testing (LV). * Good understanding of LOTO (Lock Out/Tag Out) practices, hazardous energy and permit systems * NFQ L6 (Tradesperson) or equivalent in an electrical discipline, or other technical fields will be considered. * Engineering Degree is desirable but not essential. **Why should you apply?** We’re not a perfect place – but we’re a people place. Our priority is supporting all of the different realities our people face. Life is about so much more than work. We get it. That’s why we’ve designed our total rewards to give you the flexibility to choose what you need, when you need it, making sure that you and your family are supported not only financially, but physically and emotionally too. Visit the link below to discover why we’re a great place to work and what being part of more means for you. https://www.morethanacareer.energy/cbs If you're full of energy, fired up about sustainability, and ready to craft not only a better tomorrow, but a better you, then come and find your purpose in a team where your voice matters, your growth is non\-negotiable, and your ambitions are our priority. Help us, help you. We would love for you to share any information about yourself throughout our recruitment process so that we can better understand you and help shape your journey.

title: Data Centre Technician - Technical IT (contract)
description: Please note that this is a contract role providing services to Microsoft through external staffing partners of Allegis Global Solutions (AGS). If you are selected for this role, you will be employed by AGS and will not be an employee of Microsoft. **Role Overview:** Data Centre Technician – Technical IT is an entry‑level, hands‑on data centre role focused on decommissioning hardware, cable removal, and basic physical IT tasks. The role follows clear procedures and checklists with guidance from senior technicians. This is not a scanning or advanced engineering role \- it is physical, process‑driven work inside a data centre. **Roles \& Responsibilities:** * Remove network and power cables during data centre decommissioning * Run, label, stage, and organize power and network cabling * Perform DBD removals only (no scanning or data processing) * Prepare, stage, start up, and shut down hardware using written guides * Always follow standard operating procedures and safety rules * Assist with installing, replacing, and decommissioning IT equipment * Conduct physical audits of data centre assets * Work on‑site during core business hours; flexibility for extended hours if needed **Must‑Have Skills:** * High school diploma / GED / equivalent * Basic understanding of: + Computer hardware + Servers and IT components * Ability to follow written instructions, checklists, and procedures * Working knowledge of: + Microsoft Outlook + Microsoft Excel + Microsoft Word * Comfortable with physical, hands‑on work in a data centre environment **Nice‑to‑Have Skills:** * Prior data centre or IT hardware support experience * Experience with cable management or rack/stack work * Familiarity with asset tagging or physical inventory audits * Exposure to decommissioning or migration projects **Experience Level Guidance:** * 0–2 years’ experience * Suitable for: + Entry‑level IT technicians + Field support technicians + Hardware or datacenter support associates * Not a senior or design role **Additional Details:** * Location: DUB24; D22 XF88 and DUB60; Clondalkin, Ireland (ONSITE) * Duration: 4 months 16 days * Shift Timings: Monday – Friday 8am \- 4:30pm (should be flexible work hours) * Pay Range: €29\- €32/hr (PAYE) * Weekly Schedule: 40 hours As a workplace, Allegis Global Solutions focuses on relationships – with each other, our clients and our candidates. In fact, serving others is one of our core values. We support open communication and recognize that giving constructive criticism can be even harder than receiving it. We appreciate the fearless and the passionate, who force us to be better. Everything we do sits on a pillar of diversity, equity and inclusion \- diverse perspectives, backgrounds and ideas achieve innovation and make us successful. Please note that we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position. These tools may analyse application materials and assist our team in identifying candidates whose qualifications best match the requirements of the role. If you have questions about our use of AI in the hiring process, or would like more information, please contact us.

title: Data Privacy Associate
description: **About us** At Davy, it’s the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team – you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **Requirements** **About the role** An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Data Privacy Associate within the Data \&Analytics Team at Davy Group. This role is ideal for a motivated and adaptable self\-starter who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and can deliver work to tight deadlines. The successful candidate will be a strong team player who can also take ownership of individual tasks and contribute to the organisation’s data governance and privacy objectives. This position plays a key role in supporting GDPR compliance and promoting data governance best practices across the organisation. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Manage and respond to queries related to personal data processing and handle Data Subject Rights requests within legal timeframes. * Coordinate and maintain the organisation’s Record of Processing Activities (RoPA), including regular reviews, in collaboration with stakeholders such as Privacy Champions, Legal, and Third\-Party Management, to ensure accuracy and compliance. * Coordinate and support business units in completing Privacy Assessments (PAs), ensuring they are carried out effectively, risks are identified and monitored, and outcomes are documented in collaboration with stakeholders such as Privacy Champions, Legal, and Third\-Party Management. * Utilise privacy workflow tools to manage compliance activities effectively. * Assist with the implementation of data privacy policies, supporting procedures, and activities to ensure compliance with the GDPR and best practices. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role. * Collaborate with all areas of the organisation to implement practical GDPR solutions and drive data governance best practices. * Develop strong working relationships interdepartmentally to ensure cross functional collaboration across the organisation. * Participate in projects and deliver ad hoc tasks as required. **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: **1\. Experience \& Background** * 2\+ years financial services or legal industry experience. * Basic understanding of the GDPR and data privacy principles. **2\. Technical Skills** * A high degree of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. * Experience with privacy tools, such as Microsoft Purview and OneTrust is a distinct advantage but not essential. **3\. Core Competencies** * Highly organised, with the ability to manage multiple deliverables and meet strict deadlines. * Excellent attention to detail. * Ability to prioritise workload effectively. * Ability to handle confidential information with discretion. **4\. Communication \& Collaboration** * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to deliver complex information clearly and professionally. * Proactive and collaborative approach, with confidence to build relationships and engage stakeholders across the organisation. **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today’s fast\-moving world, we know it’s important to feel secure and empowered. That’s why Davy benefits support you through all of life’s stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values – One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don’t meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Data, Commercial & Technology Lawyer
description: **OVERVIEW** The Maples Group is a standard bearer in financial and legal services, trusted by many of the world’s largest hedge fund managers, private equity firms and international corporations. Our distinction flows from our carefully curated team: 2,500\+ professionals characterised by tenacity, ethics and exacting excellence. Operating in key financial centres across the Americas, Asia, Europe and the Middle East, our international presence offers a unique springboard for career development and cross\-cultural immersion. Our side\-by\-side financial and legal services are similarly ripe for interdisciplinary learning and growth. The Maples Group looks to add a Dublin based Data, Commercial and Technology Associate to our team and invites eager and qualified candidates to apply. We are committed to diversity, inclusion and equality of opportunity as we attract, retain and develop world\-class talent. **Who We Seek** Our merit\-based culture suits professionals in pursuit of boundless careers and lives. Beyond their acumen, team members are collaborative and conscientious, bringing a healthy sense of drive and purpose to each interaction and to all aspects of their work. **About the Role** This is a fantastic opportunity to take the next step in your career and join our successful and highly collegiate Data, Commercial and Technology (DCT) team. The DCT team advises international, institutional clients and Irish established innovation driven enterprises on a broad range of matters including data protection, AI, intellectual property, technology and commercial contracts. The group sits alongside our Corporate and Employment colleagues, collaborating regularly on high profile transactions. This role offers a unique opportunity for an experienced Associate to work closely with the lead partner and take an active role in growing the practice. This role requires the ability to provide technically accurate and complex advice to clients. In addition, the successful candidate will have the ability to take the lead on advices. The successful candidate will enjoy unrivalled exposure to quality and visible transactions and will be provided the opportunity to build their profile in the market. The team is hugely collegiate and works very closely with the other Dublin practice areas to ensure a seamless and comprehensive service for its clients. **EXPERIENCE** **What You Bring** The successful candidate will have strong technical skills, a strong work ethic and a commitment to providing legal support on a wide range of complex commercial transactions. The ideal candidate will have the ability to build relationships with clients and colleagues and work well in a team environment. The candidate's qualifications and experience will also include * Between three to six years' post qualification experience * Highly relevant experience as a commercial lawyer with particular expertise on GDPR compliance and investigations, emerging technologies, IP and data aspects of joint ventures and mergers \& acquisitions. * Both experience and training should have been completed with a leading law firm in Ireland, the UK, or a major onshore jurisdiction * Outstanding academics * Excellent drafting skills * The ability to work as part of a team, supervise junior associates and take an active part in sharing knowledge and experience within the team This post requires significant attention to detail, professionalism, ability to multi\-task, and excellent organisational, prioritisation and communications skills. **BENEFITS \& REWARDS** The most enduring professional relationships are reciprocal relationships. The Maples Group prioritises employee health and wellbeing. Depending on your location, we offer a range of benefits, including: * Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental and optical) * Competitive vacation packages * Educational assistance and professional development programmes * Savings or pension plan * Life insurance * Travel insurance * Global mental wellness programme * Sports clubs and social events **ABOUT MAPLES GROUP** Over five decades, the Maples Group has grown from modest beginnings into one of the world's preeminent professional services firms, offering specialised fiduciary, fund administration, regulatory and compliance, entity formation and management and legal services on the laws of the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Ireland, Jersey and Luxembourg. You can learn more about the Maples Group on our corporate website. Experience our culture and our people on our Careers Page or on LinkedIn. Any candidates invited to interview for a role in Maples and Calder, Dublin, will receive a sensory overview of the office, inclusive of sensory maps and accessibility information. Please note that it is the Maples Group standard policy to undertake various background screening checks on all applicants to whom a conditional job offer is made. Except for roles based in the Republic of Ireland, the background checks will include criminal records checks when a conditional job offer is made. If you have a criminal record, it does not mean that your job offer will be automatically withdrawn. The Maples Group will make all job offer decisions on a case\-by\-case basis and will take a number of factors into account, such as the role that you are applying for and the nature and circumstances of the past offence. You will have the opportunity to discuss the matter with us before a decision is made. Further details will be provided at the time any conditional job offer is made. Disclaimer: All personal information collected during the application process will be used for recruitment\-related purposes only. Please refer to our Job Applicant Privacy Notice at maples.com/privacy for details on how we handle personal information relating to job applicants.

title: Databricks Data Architect
description: **EXL (NASDAQ: EXLS)** is a global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes and unlock growth with speed. EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com. **Role Title:** Databricks Data Architect **BU/Segment:** Data Management / Banking \& Financial Services **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent or Contract **Summary of the role:** Experienced **Databricks Data Architect** required to support a high\-impact data transformation initiative focused on automating reporting and dashboard processes. You will work with structured and unstructured data sources, designing and building scalable ingestion and transformation pipelines within a Databricks medallion architecture. The role will involve stakeholder engagement, defining data acquisition approaches, orchestrating bronze/silver/gold data pipelines and establishing best practices across governance, lineage, data quality, performance optimisation and release management. Strong hands\-on experience with Databricks, modern data architecture and large\-scale data processing environments is essential. **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * **Data Architecture \& Medallion Design:** * Design and evolve scalable data models within a **Databricks lakehouse architecture** , structuring data across **Bronze, Silver and Gold layers** to support reporting and dashboard automation. * **Data Pipeline Design \& Delivery:** * Lead the design and implementation of **data ingestion and transformation pipelines** , working across structured and unstructured data sources to enable reliable, production\-grade data flows. * **Resident Client Engagement:** * Act as a **resident Data Architect on client site** , working closely with stakeholders to understand requirements, shape data solutions, and ensure alignment to business outcomes. * **Requirements Gathering \& Translation:** * Engage with business and technical stakeholders to **gather, refine and define data requirements** , translating them into scalable and maintainable data architecture designs. * **Solution Evolution \& Ownership:** * Take ownership of the **ongoing evolution of the data platform** , continuously improving architecture, pipelines, and data structures in line with changing business needs. * **Team Leadership (Onshore \& Offshore):** * Lead and guide **distributed delivery teams (onshore and offshore)** , ensuring consistent standards, clear direction, and successful execution of data initiatives. * **Data Governance, Lineage \& Quality:** * Establish and enforce best practices across **data governance, lineage, metadata management, and data quality** , ensuring trust and usability of data assets. * **Performance \& Optimisation:** * Drive improvements in **data processing performance, scalability, and cost optimisation** , particularly within Databricks and distributed processing environments. * **Standards \& Documentation:** * Define and maintain **data architecture standards, models, and documentation** , ensuring consistency, reusability, and alignment across delivery teams. * **Delivery \& Release Support:** * Support **deployment and release processes** , ensuring data solutions are robust, properly governed, and production\-ready. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * **Education \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field, with **significant experience (typically 8–10\+ years)** in data architecture, data warehousing, and large\-scale data platforms, including **hands\-on experience in Databricks\-led environments** . * **Data Modelling Expertise:** * Strong experience in **enterprise data modelling** across conceptual, logical, and physical layers, ideally within **data lakehouse architectures** (tools such as ERwin, ER/Studio, PowerDesigner or equivalent). * **Databricks \& Data Platform Expertise:** * Proven hands\-on experience designing and supporting **Databricks\-based data platforms** , including medallion architecture, Spark\-based processing, and scalable data pipelines. * **Data Integration \& Processing:** * Experience delivering **data ingestion and transformation solutions** (batch and streaming) using tools such as Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), or similar modern data integration frameworks. * **Cloud \& Distributed Data Environments:** * Strong understanding of **cloud\-native data platforms** (Azure preferred), including experience working with distributed data processing and integration with enterprise data stores (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, Synapse). * **Business Intelligence \& Reporting Enablement:** * Familiarity with **reporting and visualisation tools** such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar, with an understanding of how data architecture supports analytics and dashboarding. * **Industry Experience (Desirable):** * Experience within **Insurance, Financial Services, or regulated environments** is advantageous. **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * **Delivery\-Focused Leadership:** * Proven experience **leading onshore and offshore data teams** , providing architectural direction and ensuring successful delivery within Databricks\-centric programmes. * **Strong Stakeholder Engagement:** * Ability to **engage with client stakeholders on\-site** , gather and refine requirements, and translate business needs into scalable data solutions. * **Hands\-on Architecture Mindset:** * Comfortable working in a **hands\-on capacity** , supporting teams with data modelling, pipeline design, and architectural decisions rather than purely high\-level solutioning. * **Databricks \& Modern Data Practices:** * Strong understanding of **Databricks best practices** , including medallion architecture, data pipeline design, performance optimisation, and orchestration. * **Data Governance \& Quality:** * Experience implementing and promoting **data governance, lineage, metadata, and data quality frameworks** within enterprise data environments. * **Problem Solving \& Continuous Improvement:** * Ability to **evolve and improve data solutions** in\-flight, adapting to changing business requirements and driving continuous enhancement of the data platform. **Communication \& Collaboration:** * Strong communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across **technical teams, business users, and senior stakeholders** . **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. We offer a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities. We also support a range of learning initiatives that allow our employees to build on their existing skills and knowledge. From online courses to seminars and workshops, our employees have the opportunity to enhance their skills and stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies. * As an Equal Opportunity Employer, **EXL** is committed to diversity. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Deputy Financial Controller
description: **Role Overview** Reporting to the Financial Controller, the Deputy Financial Controller plays a central role in the day\-to\-day management, reporting, and control of the organisation’s finances. The postholder deputises for the Financial Controller as required and helps drive accuracy, efficiency, and strategic insight across the business. This is an excellent opportunity for a fully ACCA qualified finance professional to develop their career in a dynamic and commercially focused environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Financial Reporting \& Control** • Prepare accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in line with applicable accounting standards. • Produce management accounts, variance analysis, and commentary for senior stakeholders. • Own the integrity of the general ledger and deliver a timely, accurate month\-end close. • Maintain and continually strengthen financial controls, policies, and procedures. **Budgeting \& Forecasting** • Lead the cashflow forecasting process, producing short and long\-term forecasts, flagging liquidity risks, and presenting findings to senior leadership. • Support the annual budget and reforecast cycles, partnering with budget holders across the business. • Deliver financial analysis and scenario modelling to inform business planning and decision\-making. • Monitor budget performance and surface risks and opportunities to the Financial Controller. **Compliance \& Audit** • Coordinate the external audit, acting as a primary point of contact for auditors. • Ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory reporting requirements, including VAT and corporation tax. • Support the implementation of robust internal audit and risk management frameworks. **Leadership \& Process Improvement** • Supervise and mentor junior members of the finance team, fostering continuous improvement and professional development. • Deputise for the Financial Controller, providing leadership and direction to the wider finance function. • Identify and implement improvements to financial systems, reporting tools, and processes, ensuring data integrity and appropriate access controls. **Person Specification** **Essential** • Fully ACCA qualified, with 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience in a senior finance role. • Proven track record in management accounts, financial reporting, and budgeting. • Demonstrated ability to manage and develop a finance team. • High level of accuracy and attention to detail, with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. • Excellent communication skills, able to present financial information clearly to non\-finance audiences. • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience with accounting and ERP systems. **Desirable** • Experience in the property or real estate sector. • Exposure to external audit coordination and statutory accounts preparation. • Working knowledge of VAT returns and corporation tax compliance. • Experience with financial systems implementation or process improvement projects. • Familiarity with applying artificial intelligence tools in a finance context. **What We Offer** • Competitive salary commensurate with experience and qualifications. • Generous annual leave plus bank holidays. • Hybrid and flexible working arrangements (role dependent). • Continuous professional development and clear career progression. • A supportive, collaborative, and ambitious working environment.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build** * Dublin Docklands, 4 days onsite, 1 day from home * €70,000 \- €110,000 base \+ bonus Our client is a global software company that has been delivering mission\-critical technology platforms for more than 45 years across healthcare, finance, logistics and government sectors. Their systems underpin large\-scale environments where uptime, scalability, resilience and security are essential. As part of a major cloud transformation programme, they’re building out a brand\-new DevOps and cloud platform engineering function in Dublin and are hiring multiple engineers across Senior, Lead and Principal\-level roles. This team will focus on designing, automating and operating large\-scale Kubernetes and cloud\-native infrastructure across AWS, Azure and GCP, supporting globally distributed SaaS and managed service platforms. Depending on your level, you’ll either be deeply hands\-on across automation, Kubernetes and infrastructure engineering, or operating as a senior technical leader shaping architecture, engineering standards, cloud strategy and long\-term platform scalability. The environment is heavily engineering\-led with a strong focus on Kubernetes at scale, Infrastructure as Code, observability, automation, platform reliability and modern DevOps practices across highly available production systems. **Non\-Negotiables** * Kubernetes in large\-scale production environments * DevOps or Platform Engineering experience across AWS, Azure and/or GCP * Terraform and Infrastructure as Code * Helm and Kubernetes package management * Strong Linux and containerisation experience * CI/CD pipelines and automation tooling * Kubernetes networking, security and observability * Experience operating highly available cloud platforms * Strong troubleshooting skills across Kubernetes and cloud environments * Experience working in complex enterprise or mission\-critical environments **What You’ll Work With** * Kubernetes * AWS / Azure / GCP * EKS / AKS / GKE * Terraform * Helm * GitOps workflows * ArgoCD / Flux * Prometheus * Grafana * Loki * Fluentd / Fluent Bit * RBAC and secrets management * Multi\-cloud and hybrid infrastructure * CI/CD and platform automation **Nice to Haves** * CKA / CKAD / CKS certifications * Service mesh technologies such as Istio, Linkerd or Cilium * Velero, Kasten or Kubernetes backup tooling * Spectro Cloud or enterprise Kubernetes platforms * SRE and reliability engineering experience * Experience mentoring engineers or setting technical standards * Open\-source Kubernetes or cloud\-native contributions * Experience supporting regulated or compliance\-heavy environments **Why Join** You’ll be joining during a significant investment into cloud\-native infrastructure and platform engineering, helping build and scale Kubernetes platforms that support critical global systems used across healthcare and other highly regulated industries. There’s strong technical ownership throughout the team. Senior engineers will influence automation, DevOps tooling and platform standards, while Principal\-level hires will shape architecture, cloud strategy and long\-term engineering direction across multi\-cloud environments. The Dublin engineering function is scaling rapidly, making this a strong opportunity for progression, visibility and the chance to help define how the DevOps and platform capability evolves over the coming years **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build**

title: Domestic Market Operations Specialist
description: Job Req Id: 26962984 Location(s): Dublin, Leinster, Ireland Job Type: Hybrid Posted: May. 14, 2026 **Discover your future at Citi** -------------------------------- Working at Citi is far more than just a job. At Citi, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your career, give back to your community and make a real impact. **Job Overview** ---------------- Agency and Trust offers a comprehensive suite of agency and fiduciary services to support issuances of debt in both domestic and international markets. The role within the Domestic Market group is a challenging fast growing operations environment where the candidate will have the opportunity to expand his/her knowledge of the different products offered within the Securities Business. The role will require the candidate to assume responsibility for day to day processing of the full Domestic Issuance and Domestic Markets teams processes. This will involve acting as a first point of contact for any queries related to the teams’ processes. **Responsibilities:** * Act as a junior member of the team to cover the following processes: Domestic Markets \& Domestic Issuance. * Daily monitoring of tasks to ensure all deliverables are completed in accordance with their corresponding cut off times. * Act as first point of contact within the team \- liaise with other teams and clients to resolve escalated problems. * Demonstrate ability to manage internal and external client relationships, to ensure the department responsibilities and objectives are met. * Review daily checklist ensuring all tasks have been signed as completed. * Complete any clerical tasks/activities within area depending on staffing levels and volumes. * Continuously develop prioritization and multi\-tasking skills, with ability to delegate work where necessary to ensure urgent and unexpected tasks are completed. * Ensuring all non\-vanilla requirements are rolled out effectively across Agency and Trust and reflected in our core system ATIS, including liaison with Issuers and Front Office to ensure full understanding of their bespoke requirements. * Complete weekly MIS reporting with justifications for volumes, aged items and fluctuations. * Ensure Department Audit Requirements and procedures are adhered to. * Ensure the
